name: Mustard Catfish Tempura
recipeInstructions:
Cut the catfish into bite-size chunks, no larger than 1/2 inch thick and 1 1/2 inches long.
In a blender or food processor, combine the egg yolks, egg, water, and salsa. Puree for five seconds. Pour into a large mixing bowl. Add the cumin and pepper. With a fork, gradually stir in the flour until incorporated but not over-stirred; there should be some flour floating on top to create a light batter.
Pat the fish dry with paper towels. Using a deep-fat fryer or electric skillet with enough oil to cover the fish completely, heat the oil to 375 degrees Fahrenheit. Dip each piece of fish into the tempura batter, then lower them gently into the hot oil. Fry for five minutes, or until golden brown. Remove cooked pieces and drain on paper towels.
